title: myrica punctata
intro: Myrica punctata, also known as prickly bay or bayberry, is a shrub or small tree native to North America, belonging to the family Myricaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Myrica punctata include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
